description: Vitamin A, also known as retinol, is an essential fat-soluble vitamin that supports immune function, vision in dim light, cellular growth, and the maintenance of healthy skin and epithelial linings such as the respiratory and digestive tract.
primary_functions: Maintains vision, especially in low-light conditions
Supports formation and maintenance of protective body barriers
supportive_roles: Strengthens natural immune defenses against pathogens
Helps maintain protective barriers in respiratory and digestive systems
Helps maintain mucosal linings such as nose and throat

absorption.notes: Vitamin A is fat-soluble and absorbs better when consumed with healthy fats such as nuts, seeds, or oils.

storage.notes: Stored in the liver for future use and released when needed.

safety_notes: Prefer natural food sources for supportive care. Excessive intake from artificial supplementation may cause toxicity. Natural food-based intake is safer and recommended.

description: Vitamin B1, also known as thiamin, is an essential water-soluble vitamin that helps the body convert food into energy and supports proper nervous system function.
functions: Helps convert carbohydrates into usable energy
Supports proper muscle and nerve coordination
supportive_roles: Supports energy levels and reduces fatigue related to deficiency
excess_causes: Excess thiamin from supplements (rare due to water-soluble nature)
absorption_notes: Vitamin B1 is water-soluble and cannot be stored in the body, so regular daily intake is required.

metadata.storage: Not stored in the body; requires daily intake

safety_notes: Prefer obtaining from natural food sources. Excess from food is generally safe because excess amounts are excreted in urine.

description: Vitamin B2, also known as riboflavin, is an essential water-soluble vitamin that supports energy production, tissue repair, and the maintenance of healthy skin, eyes, and nervous system.
functions: Helps convert food into usable cellular energy
Supports tissue repair and cellular maintenance
excess_causes: Excess riboflavin from supplementation (rare due to water-soluble nature)
absorption_notes: Vitamin B2 is water-soluble and cannot be stored in the body. UV light can destroy riboflavin in foods, so proper storage away from direct sunlight is important.

metadata.storage: Not stored in the body; requires daily intake

safety_notes: Prefer obtaining from natural food sources. Excess from food is generally safe because excess amounts are excreted in urine.

description: Vitamin B3, also known as niacin, is an essential water-soluble vitamin that supports cellular energy production, nervous system stability, DNA repair, and the maintenance of healthy skin.
functions: Helps convert food into cellular energy
Supports DNA repair and cellular maintenance
absorption_notes: Vitamin B3 is water-soluble and cannot be stored in the body, so regular daily intake is required. The body can also synthesize small amounts from the amino acid tryptophan.

metadata.storage: Not stored in the body; requires daily intake

safety_notes: Prefer obtaining from natural food sources. Excessive supplementation may cause skin flushing and liver stress, so supplementation should be approached cautiously.

description: Vitamin B5, also known as pantothenic acid, is an essential water-soluble vitamin that supports energy metabolism, fatty acid synthesis, and cellular metabolic processes necessary for growth, repair, and overall physiological function.
functions: Supports metabolism of carbohydrates, proteins, and fats
excess_causes: Excess intake from supplementation (rare due to water-soluble nature)

recommended_daily_allowance.adult_male: Not officially established (adequate intake met through diet)
recommended_daily_allowance.adult_female: Not officially established (adequate intake met through diet)

absorption_notes: Vitamin B5 is water-soluble and cannot be stored in the body, so regular dietary intake is required. It plays a key role in coenzyme A production, which is essential for metabolic reactions.

metadata.storage: Not stored in the body; requires daily intake

safety_notes: Vitamin B5 is generally safe when obtained from natural food sources. Excess intake from food is typically excreted and does not accumulate.

description: Vitamin B6, also known as pyridoxine, is an essential water-soluble vitamin that supports protein metabolism, hemoglobin formation, neurotransmitter synthesis, and immune system function.
functions: Supports metabolism of proteins and carbohydrates
Supports oxygen transport in the blood
supportive_roles: Supports brain function and neurological stability
excess_causes: Vitamin B6 Toxicity from excessive supplementation
absorption_notes: Vitamin B6 is water-soluble and cannot be stored in large amounts in the body, so regular intake is required. Gut bacteria can also produce small amounts. Excessive supplementation may cause nerve damage.
safety_notes: Prefer natural food sources. Excessive supplementation may cause peripheral neuropathy and nerve damage, especially at high doses over long periods.

description: Vitamin B7, also known as biotin, is an essential water-soluble vitamin that supports fatty acid synthesis, energy metabolism, and the maintenance of healthy skin, hair, and cellular function.
functions: Supports metabolism of carbohydrates, fats, and proteins
excess_causes: Excess intake from supplementation (effects not clearly established)
absorption_notes: Vitamin B7 is water-soluble and required in small amounts. Gut bacteria naturally produce biotin, but dietary intake helps maintain optimal levels.

metadata.storage: Not stored in large amounts; requires regular intake

safety_notes: Biotin is generally safe when obtained from natural food sources. The body and gut bacteria produce small amounts, and excess is typically excreted.

description: Vitamin B9, also known as folate (natural form) or folic acid (synthetic form), is an essential water-soluble vitamin that supports DNA synthesis, red blood cell formation, cell division, and nervous system development.
functions: Supports formation of healthy red blood cells
excess_causes: Potential nervous system damage if B12 deficiency is undetected
absorption_notes: Vitamin B9 is water-soluble and not stored in large amounts, requiring regular dietary intake. Synthetic folic acid is more easily absorbed than natural folate.
supportive_use_cases: Support for cellular repair and regeneration
safety_notes: Prefer natural food sources. Excessive folic acid supplementation may mask Vitamin B12 deficiency and delay proper diagnosis.

description: Vitamin B12, also known as cobalamin, is an essential water-soluble vitamin that supports red blood cell formation, prevents anemia, maintains nervous system health, supports DNA synthesis, and helps the body release energy from food.
primary_functions: Supports DNA synthesis and cellular health
supportive_roles: Supports oxygen transport through healthy red blood cells
deficiency_effects: Tingling or numbness in hands and feet

inhibited_by: Strict vegan diet without fortified foods

absorption_notes: Vitamin B12 absorption requires intrinsic factor produced in the stomach. It is primarily obtained from animal-based foods or fortified foods.

metadata.storage: Stored in the liver for long-term use

safety_notes: Vitamin B12 is generally safe when obtained from natural food sources. Deficiency is more common in individuals who avoid animal products or have absorption issues.

description: Vitamin C, also known as ascorbic acid, is an essential water-soluble vitamin that protects cells from oxidative damage, supports immune system function, promotes collagen production, maintains healthy skin, blood vessels, bones, and cartilage, and supports wound healing.
primary_functions: Supports collagen synthesis and tissue repair
Supports skin and structural tissue health
deficiency_causes: Insufficient intake of fruits and vegetables
absorption_notes: Vitamin C is water-soluble and cannot be stored in significant amounts in the body. Regular dietary intake is required to maintain adequate levels.

metadata.storage: Not stored in significant amounts; requires regular intake

safety_notes: Vitamin C is generally safe when consumed from natural food sources. Excessive supplementation above 1000 mg per day may cause digestive discomfort such as stomach pain, diarrhea, and flatulence.

description: Vitamin D is an essential fat-soluble vitamin that regulates calcium and phosphate balance in the body, supports bone and teeth strength, enhances muscle function, and supports immune system function. It is uniquely synthesized in the skin through sunlight exposure and also obtained from certain foods and supplements.
absorption_notes: Vitamin D is synthesized in the skin through sunlight exposure and absorbed from dietary sources. Being fat-soluble, it requires dietary fat for optimal absorption and can be stored in body tissues.

metadata.storage: Stored in fat tissues and liver for long-term use

safety_notes: Vitamin D is safe when obtained from sunlight and dietary sources. Excessive supplementation above 100 µg (4000 IU) per day can cause hypercalcemia, leading to kidney damage, heart problems, and bone weakening.

description: Vitamin E is an essential fat-soluble antioxidant that helps protect cells from oxidative damage, maintains healthy skin and eyes, and strengthens the immune system. It plays a critical role in protecting cellular membranes and supporting immune defense mechanisms.
absorption_notes: Vitamin E is a fat-soluble vitamin that requires dietary fat for proper absorption and can be stored in body tissues for future use.
supportive_use_cases: Support for recovery from oxidative stress

metadata.storage: Stored in fat tissues and liver for future use

safety_notes: Vitamin E is safe when obtained from natural food sources. Excess supplementation effects are not clearly established, but extremely high doses may interfere with normal blood clotting.

description: Vitamin K is an essential fat-soluble vitamin that plays a critical role in blood clotting, wound healing, and bone health. It helps activate proteins required for proper coagulation and supports bone strength by regulating calcium utilization.
supportive_roles: Supports healing of injuries and wounds
absorption_notes: Vitamin K is a fat-soluble vitamin that requires dietary fats for proper absorption and is stored in the liver for future use.

metadata.storage: Stored in the liver for future use

safety_notes: Vitamin K is generally safe when obtained from natural food sources. Excess supplementation effects are not clearly established.

description: Calcium is an essential mineral that plays a critical role in building and maintaining strong bones and teeth, regulating muscle contractions including heart function, supporting blood clotting, and enabling proper nerve signaling.
primary_functions: Supports bone health and skeletal integrity
Supports muscle contraction and heart function
supportive_roles: Supports prevention of osteoporosis and osteomalacia
absorption_notes: Calcium absorption is significantly enhanced by Vitamin D. Adequate Vitamin D levels are essential for proper calcium utilization.

metadata.storage: Stored primarily in bones and teeth

safety_notes: Calcium is safe when obtained from natural food sources. Excess supplementation above recommended levels may cause stomach discomfort and digestive issues.

description: Iodine is an essential trace mineral required for the production of thyroid hormones, which regulate metabolism, cellular function, growth, and energy balance throughout the body.
deficiency_causes: Strict vegan diet without fortified foods
absorption_notes: Iodine is efficiently absorbed from food and is primarily used by the thyroid gland to produce thyroid hormones.
supportive_use_cases: Support for fatigue related to thyroid dysfunction
safety_notes: Iodine is safe when obtained from natural food sources. Excessive supplementation may disrupt thyroid function and cause metabolic imbalances.

description: Iron is an essential mineral required for the production of hemoglobin in red blood cells, enabling oxygen transport throughout the body. It plays a critical role in preventing anemia, supporting energy production, and maintaining overall cellular function.
functions: Enables oxygen transport throughout the body
primary_functions: Supports prevention of iron deficiency anemia
supportive_roles: Supports energy levels and fatigue reduction
absorption_notes: Iron absorption is significantly enhanced when consumed with vitamin C-rich foods. Animal-based iron (heme iron) is absorbed more efficiently than plant-based iron (non-heme iron).
supportive_use_cases: Support for recovery from blood loss

metadata.storage: Stored in the liver, spleen, and bone marrow

safety_notes: Iron is safe when obtained from natural food sources. Excessive supplementation may cause digestive distress and toxicity. Iron supplements should be used cautiously and kept away from children.

description: Potassium is an essential mineral and electrolyte that regulates fluid balance, supports heart function, maintains proper nerve signaling, and enables normal muscle contraction. It plays a critical role in maintaining blood pressure and overall cellular stability.
absorption_notes: Potassium is efficiently absorbed from natural food sources and helps maintain electrical balance across cell membranes.
safety_notes: Potassium is safe when obtained from natural food sources. Excessive intake, especially from supplements, may cause digestive discomfort or affect heart rhythm.

description: Protein is an essential macronutrient required for the growth, repair, and maintenance of body tissues. It is made up of amino acids that support muscle development, immune function, enzyme and hormone production, and proper functioning of organs such as the brain, heart, and liver.
supportive_roles: Supports recovery from illness or injury
absorption_notes: Protein is broken down into amino acids during digestion and absorbed in the small intestine, where they are used to build and repair body tissues.
supportive_use_cases: Support for overall strength and health

metadata.storage: Stored as amino acids and in body tissues

safety_notes: Protein is safe when consumed from natural food sources. Excessive intake, especially from supplements, may cause digestive discomfort or kidney strain in extreme cases.

description: Fats are essential macronutrients that provide energy, support cell structure, protect organs, and enable the absorption of fat-soluble vitamins such as vitamins A, D, and E. They also supply essential fatty acids required for brain function, hormone production, and cardiovascular health.
supportive_roles: Supports cardiovascular health when healthy fats are consumed
absorption_notes: Fats are digested in the small intestine with the help of bile and enzymes, allowing absorption of essential fatty acids and fat-soluble vitamins.

metadata.storage: Stored in adipose (fat) tissue for energy reserve

safety_notes: Fats are essential for health when consumed in balanced amounts. Excessive intake of saturated and trans fats increases the risk of heart disease and cholesterol imbalance, while healthy unsaturated fats support cardiovascular health.

description: Carbohydrates are essential macronutrients that serve as the body's primary source of energy. They are broken down into glucose, which fuels cellular activity, supports brain function, and maintains overall metabolic processes. Carbohydrates include simple sugars and complex starches, which differ in their digestion speed and impact on blood glucose levels.
functions: Provides primary energy for the body
supportive_roles: Supports glycogen storage for future energy use
absorption_notes: Carbohydrates are broken down into glucose in the digestive system. Insulin helps transport glucose into cells where it is used for energy or stored as glycogen.

metadata.storage: Stored as glycogen in liver and muscles

safety_notes: Carbohydrates are essential for energy production. Balanced intake from complex carbohydrate sources such as whole grains, fruits, and vegetables supports metabolic health, while excessive intake of refined sugars may increase the risk of metabolic disorders.
